body_txt: A character performs in a play as an inanimate object, commonly on stages. It's often used to undermine a character, making them the Butt-Monkey of the moment. Sometimes they'll get One-Line Anxiety anyway, demonstrating them to be very strange or delusional. Obviously, having actors essentially playing props would be completely pointless in a real production, but for a Work Within A Work , it's fine because of the Rule of Funny . It may be justified if it's a School Play production in which everyone in the class needs to have something to do, and the adults in charge are forced to stretch the definitions of "actor" and "character", which actually does happen in Real Life (especially in certain cases like The Wizard Of Oz where having people playing the background scenery Makes Sense In Context ). Related tropes include the Living Prop (when a living person is basically a prop, but isn't literally portraying an inanimate object) and The Living Dead (when an actor gets the inanimate role of a corpse), but note that both of those pages skew towards non- Work Within A Work examples (since the role of "unimportant, but stable Ghost Extra " isn't as funny as "tree"). Not to Be Confused with literally becoming a tree , disguising as one , or using wooden instruments.
